name: llama-diffusion
description: DiffusionGemma 26B-A4B (text diffusion LLM) served locally with an OpenAI-compatible API.

install_flow.step1_ask_user: BEFORE installing, ASK the user two things: (a) which quantization to download — present the 'models.variants' list and the VRAM each needs; (b) the maximum context tokens (default 262144 = model max; Hermes requires >= 64000). Do not assume defaults silently.
install_flow.step3_install: pwsh scripts/install.ps1 -ModelFile <chosen_file> -MaxContext <chosen_ctx>   (clones+patches the engine, builds CUDA, installs deps, downloads the model, writes config.json)
install_flow.step4_serve: pwsh scripts/serve.ps1 -BindHost 0.0.0.0   (or, as admin, pwsh scripts/install_service.ps1 — auto-start service on 0.0.0.0:8787 + opens the firewall)
install_flow.step5_configure_self: Register ONLY the provider in your own config (see 'configure'). Do NOT hand-register the model: its id and context_length are auto-discovered via GET /v1/models.

models.note: vram_gb_approx = model size + ~2 GB compute buffer + KV-cache. KV is small (sliding-window attention) so VRAM is nearly context-independent here.

api.wsl_host_hint: From WSL, {HOST_IP} is the 'vEthernet (WSL)' adapter IP on the Windows host (e.g. 172.18.160.1).
api.discovery: GET /v1/models returns each model id plus its context_length / max_model_len — so registering the provider is enough; the model is found automatically.

configure.note: Register ONLY the provider (base_url). The model id and its context are discovered via GET /v1/models — never pin a context_length by hand (a wrong value, e.g. 32768, makes Hermes reject the model for being below its 64000 minimum).

hermes.action: Add ONLY a provider (type 'custom', OpenAI-compatible) with base_url set to the endpoint. No 'models' block — Hermes discovers the model and its context via /v1/models.
hermes.example:
providers:
  diffusiongemma:
    base_url: http://172.18.160.1:8787/v1
    api_key: not-needed
hermes.from_wsl: Use the Windows host vEthernet (WSL) IP for base_url (e.g. http://172.18.160.1:8787/v1).

openclaw.action: Register ONLY an OpenAI-compatible provider pointing at base_url. The model is auto-discovered via /v1/models; do not pin model metadata.

generic_openai.model: optional — omit to use the loaded model, or read it from GET /v1/models
